SEARCH
Package client.view

Class MainShellController

java.lang.Object
client.view.MainShellController
public class MainShellController extends Object
Controls the persistent shell: sidebar nav, topbar title, connection pill, the logged-in user chrome, and the contentArea swap point. All screen-specific behaviour lives in the per-screen controllers that Navigator loads. Role Aware: The sidebar is built from navItems(), each entry carrying a Predicate over the Session that decides whether the current identity may see it. Only the screens that pass are registered with the Navigator and given a button, so a hidden screen is also unreachable (not just invisible). Visitors and staff share the reservation screens; role-specific predicates (SERVICE_REP_ONLY, PARK_EMPLOYEE_ONLY, DEPT_MANAGER_ONLY, …) gate the staff tools.

    private static final record 
    MainShellController.NavItem
    One sidebar entry: the screen plus the rule deciding who may see it.

  • Constructor Details

    • MainShellController

      public MainShellController(NetworkService network, Session session, Runnable onLogout)
      Creates the main-shell controller.
      Parameters:
      network - the shared network service
      session - the current client session
      onLogout - callback invoked when the user logs out

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      private void initialize()
      FXML lifecycle hook: builds the role-filtered sidebar and user chrome.

    • navItems

      private List<MainShellController.NavItem> navItems()
      The full catalogue of shell screens with their visibility rules. Order here is the sidebar order. Future screens slot in with the appropriate predicate; non-existent screens simply aren't listed yet.
      Returns:
      the ordered catalogue of shell screens with their visibility rules

    • onLogout

      private void onLogout()
      Logout-button handler: tells the server to release the lock, then logs out.

    • finishLogout

      private void finishLogout()
      Drops the notification subscription, clears the identity, and returns to login.

    • subscribeToNotifications

      private void subscribeToNotifications()
      Registers interest in the logged-in actor's own notifications. Sends a fire-and-forget SUBSCRIBE for ("notification", actorId) and attaches a local EventBus callback. Unlike per-screen BaseController subscriptions, this one lives for the whole login (across screen swaps), so it is managed here in the shell rather than drained on navigation.

    • unsubscribeFromNotifications

      private void unsubscribeFromNotifications()
      Detaches the notification subscription and tells the server to stop pushing.

    • onNotificationEvent

      private void onNotificationEvent(ServerEvent ev)
      Handles a pushed notification event (already marshalled onto the FX thread by EventBus). Shows the simulation popup for the carried NotificationDTO; ignores events without the expected payload.
      Parameters:
      ev - the pushed notification event

    • showNotificationPopup

      private void showNotificationPopup(NotificationDTO n)
      Shows a non-blocking, auto-dismissing popup with the simulation text "Simulation — would send via <channel> to <target>: <body>".

      Deliberately uses a non-modal, undecorated Stage (not Alert.showAndWait()) so it never freezes the FX event loop or blocks interaction with the shell. A daemon timer closes it after a few seconds.

      Parameters:
      n - the notification to render

    • initialsOf

      private String initialsOf(String name)
      First letters of the first two name tokens, e.g. "Dana Department" → "DD".
      Parameters:
      name - the display name
      Returns:
      up to two uppercase initials, or "?" if blank

    • onScreenChange

      private void onScreenChange(Navigator.Screen s)
      Updates the topbar title/subtitle when the active screen changes.
      Parameters:
      s - the newly shown screen

    • updateConnStatus

      private void updateConnStatus(boolean ok)
      Reflects a connection-state change in the pill.
      Parameters:
      ok - whether the socket is currently connected

    • setConnDotOk

      private void setConnDotOk(boolean ok)
      Sets the connection dot's ok/error styling.
      Parameters:
      ok - whether to show the ok (vs error) state
